glosses.vi: nữ cảnh sát; nữ công an
glosses.ja: 女性警察官; 女警官

sentences.target: Meine Schwester ist Polizistin in Berlin.

translations.de: Meine Schwester ist Polizistin in Berlin.
translations.en: My sister is a policewoman in Berlin.
translations.es: Mi hermana es policía en Berlín.
translations.fr: Ma sœur est policière à Berlin.
translations.it: Mia sorella è poliziotta a Berlino.
translations.ru: Моя сестра работает полицейской в Берлине.
translations.pt: Minha irmã é policial em Berlim.
translations.vi: Em gái tôi là cảnh sát ở Berlin.
translations.nl: Mijn zus is politieagente in Berlijn.
translations.hi: मेरी बहन बर्लिन में पुलिसकर्मी है.
translations.pl: Moja siostra jest policjantką w Berlinie.
translations.el: Η αδελφή μου είναι αστυνομικός στο Βερολίνο.
translations.nb: Søsteren min er politi i Berlin.
translations.id: Adik perempuan saya adalah polisi di Berlin.
translations.sv: Min syster är polis i Berlin.
translations.ro: Sora mea este polițistă în Berlin.
translations.cs: Moje sestra je policistka v Berlíně.
translations.da: Min søster er politibetjent i Berlin.
translations.ja: 妹はベルリンで警察官です。

tokens.explanation: Possessive determiner (1st person singular) modifying 'Schwester'; agrees in gender, number, and case.
Common noun; head of the subject noun phrase.
Copular auxiliary linking the subject to the predicate nominal.
Predicate nominative (profession) as complement of 'ist'.
Preposition introducing a locative prepositional phrase.
Proper noun; object of the preposition 'in', indicating location.

sentences.target: Ich frage die Polizistin nach dem Weg.
sentences.reading: /ɪç ˈfʁaːɡə diː po.liˈtsɪs.tɪn naːx deːm veːk/

translations.de: Ich frage die Polizistin nach dem Weg.
translations.ipa: /ɪç ˈfʁaːɡə diː po.liˈtsɪs.tɪn naːx deːm veːk/
translations.en: I ask the policewoman for directions.
translations.es: Le pregunto a la policía cómo llegar.
translations.fr: Je demande mon chemin à la policière.
translations.pt: Eu peço informações para a policial.
translations.he: אני שואל את השוטרת איך להגיע.
translations.vi: Tôi hỏi nữ cảnh sát đường đi.
translations.nl: Ik vraag de politieagente de weg.
translations.hi: मैं पुलिसवाली से रास्ता पूछता हूँ.
translations.el: Ρωτάω την αστυνομικό για τον δρόμο.
translations.id: Saya bertanya arah kepada polisi wanita.
translations.ja: 私は女性警官に道を聞きます。

tokens.explanation: Personal pronoun serving as the subject (nominative).
Main verb in present 1st person singular; selects a direct object and a 'nach' + dative PP.
Definite article marking 'Polizistin' as accusative singular feminine.
Common noun; direct object of 'frage' (accusative).
Preposition governing dative; introduces the PP meaning 'ask for/about'.
Definite article, dative singular masculine, modifying 'Weg'.
Common noun; object of the preposition 'nach' (dative).

sentences.target: Die Polizistin hilft dem Kind über die Straße.
sentences.reading: /diː po.liˈtsɪs.tɪn hɪlft deːm kɪnt ˈyːbɐ diː ˈʃtʁaːsə/

translations.de: Die Polizistin hilft dem Kind über die Straße.
translations.ipa: /diː po.liˈtsɪs.tɪn hɪlft deːm kɪnt ˈyːbɐ diː ˈʃtʁaːsə/
translations.en: The policewoman helps the child cross the street.
translations.es: La policía ayuda a un niño a cruzar la calle.
translations.fr: La policière aide l'enfant à traverser la rue.
translations.it: La poliziotta aiuta il bambino ad attraversare la strada.
translations.pt: A policial ajuda a criança a atravessar a rua.
translations.he: השוטרת עוזרת לילד לחצות את הכביש.
translations.tr: Kadın polis çocuğun karşıdan karşıya geçmesine yardım ediyor.
translations.vi: Nữ cảnh sát giúp đứa trẻ qua đường.
translations.ar: تساعد الشرطية الطفل على عبور الشارع.
translations.nl: De politieagente helpt het kind de straat over.
translations.hu: A rendőrnő átsegíti a gyereket az úton.
translations.hi: पुलिसवाली बच्चे को सड़क पार कराने में मदद करती है.
translations.pl: Policjantka pomaga dziecku przejść przez ulicę.
translations.el: Η αστυνομικός βοηθά το παιδί να περάσει τον δρόμο.
translations.id: Polisi wanita membantu anak menyeberang jalan.
translations.ro: Polițista îl ajută pe copil să traverseze strada.
translations.ja: 女性警官は子どもが道路を渡るのを手伝います。

tokens.explanation: Definite article marking the subject NP 'Polizistin' (nominative singular feminine).
Common noun; grammatical subject.
Main verb in present 3rd person singular; takes a dative complement and a directional PP.
Definite article, dative singular, modifying 'Kind'.
Common noun; dative complement of 'hilft' (recipient).
Preposition governing accusative to indicate movement across; introduces a directional PP.
Definite article, accusative singular feminine, modifying 'Straße'.
Common noun; object of the preposition 'über', denoting the path.

sentences.target: Unser Nachbar ist mit einer Polizistin zusammen.
sentences.reading: /ˈʊnzɐ ˈnaχbaʁ ɪst mɪt ˈaɪ̯nɐ po.liˈtsɪs.tɪn tsuˈzamən/

translations.de: Unser Nachbar ist mit einer Polizistin zusammen.
translations.ipa: /ˈʊnzɐ ˈnaχbaʁ ɪst mɪt ˈaɪ̯nɐ po.liˈtsɪs.tɪn tsuˈzamən/
translations.en: Our neighbor is dating a policewoman.
translations.es: Nuestro vecino sale con una policía.
translations.fr: Notre voisin sort avec une policière.
translations.it: Il nostro vicino esce con una poliziotta.
translations.zh-Hans: 我们邻居在和一位女警交往。
translations.yue: 我哋鄰居同一位女警拍拖。
translations.pt: Nosso vizinho está namorando uma policial.
translations.vi: Hàng xóm của chúng tôi đang hẹn hò với một nữ cảnh sát.
translations.nl: Onze buurman gaat uit met een politieagente.
translations.hi: हमारा पड़ोसी एक पुलिसवाली को डेट कर रहा है.
translations.pl: Nasz sąsiad spotyka się z policjantką.
translations.el: Ο γείτονάς μας βγαίνει με μια αστυνομικό.
translations.nb: Naboen vår er sammen med en politikvinne.
translations.id: Tetangga kami sedang berpacaran dengan seorang polisi wanita.
translations.ro: Vecinul nostru se întâlnește cu o polițistă.
translations.da: Vores nabo er kærester med en kvindelig politibetjent.
translations.ja: うちの隣人は女性警官と付き合っています。

tokens.explanation: Possessive determiner (1st person plural) modifying 'Nachbar'.
Common noun; grammatical subject.
Copular auxiliary linking the subject to the predicate.
Preposition governing dative; introduces the partner PP.
Indefinite article, dative singular feminine, modifying 'Polizistin'.
Common noun; object of 'mit' indicating the person one is together with.
Adverb functioning as a predicate complement with 'sein', indicating relationship status.
